# COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist

Document ID: COMP-001
Document Type: Compliance Checklist
Assets Covered: P-101, C-201, HX-301
Review Date: 2026-03-10
Reviewer: Demo Compliance Officer
Checklist Status: Open Gaps Found

---

## 1. Compliance Context

This checklist evaluates whether required maintenance, inspection, safety, and evidence records are available for PlantMind demo assets.

The compliance logic is inspired by industrial maintenance safety practices, including:

* Work permit discipline
* Lockout tagout evidence
* Inspection evidence
* Maintenance verification
* Cleaning records
* Safe shutdown procedure availability
* Asset-specific evidence tracking

This checklist is synthetic demo data and is not a replacement for any official safety standard.

---

## 2. Assets Covered

| Asset ID | Asset Name | Asset Type | Criticality |
| -------- | ------------------------------ | -------------- | ----------- |
| P-101 | Cooling Water Circulation Pump | Pump | High |
| C-201 | Process Air Compressor | Compressor | Medium-High |
| HX-301 | Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger | Heat Exchanger | Medium |

---

## 3. Compliance Matrix

| Asset ID | Requirement | Expected Evidence | Status | Evidence File | Gap |
| -------- | ------------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------- | ------------- | --- |
| P-101 | Weekly vibration inspection | Inspection report | Available | IR-P101-001 | No |
| P-101 | Weekly lubrication check | Lubrication record | Missing | Not available | Yes |
| P-101 | Work permit before bearing inspection | Work permit record | Missing | Not available | Yes |
| C-201 | Monthly compressor inspection | Inspection report | Available | IR-C201-001 | No |
| C-201 | Filter replacement verification | Maintenance log or work order | Delayed | WO-1003 | Yes |
| C-201 | Safe shutdown procedure confirmation | SOP reference | Available | SOP-C201-001 | No |
| HX-301 | Pressure test evidence | Test record or SOP reference | Available | SOP-HX301-001 | No |
|

SOP reference | Available | SOP-C201-001 | No |
| HX-301 | Pressure test evidence | Test record or SOP reference | Available | SOP-HX301-001 | No |
| HX-301 | Cleaning and fouling inspection | Cleaning record | Overdue | Not available | Yes |
| HX-301 | Work permit before cleaning | Work permit record | Missing | Not available | Yes |

---

## 4. Ground Truth Compliance Gaps

The following compliance gaps are intentionally defined for the demo:

1. P-101 lubrication evidence is missing.
2. P-101 work permit evidence is missing before bearing inspection.
3. C-201 filter replacement verification is delayed.
4. HX-301 cleaning and fouling inspection evidence is overdue.
5. HX-301 work permit evidence is missing before cleaning activity.

---
## 5. Asset-Level Compliance Summary

### P-101 Pump

P-101 has two compliance gaps:

* Weekly lubrication evidence is missing.
* Work permit evidence is missing before bearing inspection.

P-101 also has technical risk because vibration exceeded the critical threshold.

### C-201 Compressor

C-201 has one compliance gap:

* Filter replacement verification is delayed.

Monthly compressor inspection evidence is available.

### HX-301 Heat Exchanger

HX-301 has two compliance gaps:

* Cleaning and fouling inspection evidence is overdue.
* Work permit evidence is missing before cleaning activity.

HX-301 also has technical risk because outlet temperature is below target and pressure drop is above warning threshold.

---

## 6. Recommended Compliance Actions

| Gap | Recommended Action |
| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| P-101 lubrication evidence missing | Perform lubrication check and upload lubrication record |
| P-101 work permit missing | Create or attach work permit before bearing inspection |
| C-201 filter verification delayed | Verify filter replacement completion and update work order |
| HX-301 cleaning evidence overdue | Schedule cleaning inspection and upload cleaning record |
| HX-301 work permit missing | Create or attach work permit before cleaning activity |

---

## 7. Related Documents

* SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
* SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
* SOP-P101-002: Pump Vibration Inspection
* SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
* SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
* IR-P101-001: Pump Vibration Inspection
* IR-C201-001: Compressor Monthly Inspection
* IR-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Performance Inspection
* IR-GEN-001: Safety Evidence Audit
* work_orders.csv
* ground_truth_compliance_gaps.csv

---

## 8. Keywords

compliance, compliance checklist, missing evidence, overdue evidence, delayed verification, work permit, LOTO, lubrication evidence, cleaning evidence, filter replacement, P-101, C-201, HX-301

# INC-HX301-001: Low Heat Transfer Efficiency

Document ID: INC-HX301-001
Asset ID: HX-301
Asset Name: Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger
Asset Type: Heat Exchanger
Document Type: Incident Report
Incident Date: 2026-03-07
Reported By: Operations Team
Incident Status: Open for Maintenance Review

---

## 1. Incident Summary

HX-301 Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger showed reduced heat transfer efficiency during process operation.

The incident was reported after outlet temperature dropped below the acceptable target and pressure drop increased above the warning threshold.

---

## 2. Asset Involved

Asset ID: HX-301
Asset Name: Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger
Asset Type: Shell and Tube Heat Exchanger
Location: Process Area - Unit C
Criticality: Medium

---

## 3. Observed Symptoms

The following symptoms were observed:

* Outlet temperature was below acceptable target.
* Pressure drop was above warning threshold.
* Heat transfer efficiency index was below normal range.
* Cleaning evidence was overdue.
* Fouling or restricted flow was suspected.

---

## 4. Evidence Available

The incident is supported by the following evidence:

| Evidence | Source |
| --------------------------------------- | ------------ |
| Outlet temperature recorded at 88°C | IR-HX301-001 |
| Pressure drop recorded at 1.9 bar | IR-HX301-001 |
| Efficiency index recorded at 0.71 | IR-HX301-001 |
| Cleaning evidence overdue | COMP-001 |
| Cleaning inspection work order required | WO-1005 |

---

## 5. Suspected Causes

Possible causes include:

1. Tube-side fouling
2. Shell-side fouling
3. Flow restriction
4. Delayed cleaning
5. Process-side operating variation

---

## 6. Temporary Action Taken

The operations team continued monitoring HX-301 under controlled operating conditions.

No permanent corrective action was completed at the time of this incident report.

---

## 7. Required Follow-Up

The following follow-up actions are required:

1. Schedule cleaning inspection.
2. Review last cleaning record.
3. Verify outlet temperature trend.
4. Verify pressure drop trend.
5. Inspect tube-side condition if accessible.
6. Attach cleaning evidence after maintenance.
7. Attach work permit evidence before cleaning activity.

---

## 8. Recommended Corrective Action

The maintenance team should schedule cleaning inspection for HX-301 and review fouling indicators.

If fouling is confirmed, cleaning should be completed and cleaning evidence must be attached to the asset record.

---

## 9. RCA Requirement

Root Cause Analysis is recommended because:

* Outlet temperature is below acceptable target.
* Pressure drop is above warning threshold.
* Cleaning evidence is overdue.
* Fouling or restricted flow is suspected.

---

## 10. Related Documents

* SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
* SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
* IR-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Performance Inspection
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* work_orders.csv
* ground_truth_compliance_gaps.csv

---

## 11. Keywords

HX-301, heat exchanger, low heat transfer efficiency, fouling, pressure drop, outlet temperature, cleaning overdue, incident report, RCA, compliance gap

# INC-P101-001: High Vibration Event

Document ID: INC-P101-001
Asset ID: P-101
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump
Asset Type: Pump
Document Type: Incident Report
Incident Date: 2026-01-22
Reported By: Operations Team
Incident Status: Open for RCA

---

## 1. Incident Summary

P-101 Cooling Water Circulation Pump showed repeated high vibration during normal operation.

The incident was reported after vibration readings exceeded the critical threshold and abnormal bearing noise was observed near the bearing housing.

---

## 2. Asset Involved

Asset ID: P-101
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump
Asset Type: Centrifugal Pump
Location: Utility Area - Line A
Criticality: High

---

## 3. Observed Symptoms

The following symptoms were observed:

* Vibration exceeded the critical threshold.
* Abnormal bearing noise was observed.
* Bearing temperature was slightly elevated.
* Lubrication evidence was missing for the current week.
* Operator reported intermittent vibration during normal load.

---

## 4. Evidence Available

The incident is supported by the following evidence:

| Evidence | Source |
| ----------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| Vibration reading of 7.8 mm/s | IR-P101-001 |
| Bearing temperature of 78°C | IR-P101-002 |
| Missing lubrication evidence | COMP-001 |
| Abnormal bearing noise | Maintenance observation |
| Work order required | WO-1001 |

---

## 5. Suspected Causes

Possible causes include:

1. Bearing wear
2. Lubrication degradation
3. Shaft misalignment
4. Loose mounting
5. Mechanical imbalance

---

## 6. Temporary Action Taken

The operations team reduced pump load and informed the maintenance team.

No permanent corrective action was completed at the time of this incident report.

---

## 7. Required Follow-Up

The following follow-up actions are required:

1. Generate RCA for P-101 high vibration.
2. Inspect bearing housing.
3. Verify lubrication level and lubricant quality.
4. Check shaft alignment.
5. Inspect mounting bolts.
6. Attach missing lubrication evidence.
7. Attach work permit evidence before physical bearing inspection.

---

## 8. Recommended Corrective Action

The maintenance team should create an urgent maintenance work order for P-101 bearing inspection and lubrication verification.

If bearing damage is confirmed, bearing replacement should be planned during the next safe maintenance window.

---

## 9. RCA Requirement

Root Cause Analysis is required because:

* Vibration exceeded the critical threshold.
* The issue is related to a high-criticality rotating asset.
* Multiple symptoms indicate possible bearing wear.
* Compliance evidence is missing.

---

## 10. Related Documents

* SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
* SOP-P101-002: Pump Vibration Inspection
* IR-P101-001: Pump Vibration Inspection
* IR-P101-002: Pump Bearing Temperature Check
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* work_orders.csv
* ground_truth_compliance_gaps.csv

---

## 11. Keywords

P-101, pump, high vibration, incident report, bearing wear, lubrication degradation, bearing noise, RCA, compliance gap, work order

# IR-C201-001: Compressor Monthly Inspection

Document ID: IR-C201-001
Asset ID: C-201
Asset Name: Process Air Compressor
Asset Type: Compressor
Document Type: Inspection Report
Inspection Date: 2026-02-15
Inspector: Demo Inspector
Inspection Status: Completed

---

## 1. Inspection Purpose

This inspection was performed as part of the monthly condition review for C-201 Process Air Compressor.

The inspection focused on compressor operating behavior, outlet temperature, pressure ratio stability, filter replacement verification, and maintenance planning.

---

## 2. Inspection Scope

The inspection covered:

* Outlet temperature trend
* Pressure ratio stability
* Estimated Remaining Useful Life trend
* Filter replacement verification
* Maintenance planning requirement
* Safe shutdown procedure reference

---

## 3. Measurement Summary

| Parameter | Measured Value | Normal Range | Warning Limit | Critical Limit | Status |
| ------------------ | -------------: | --------------: | --------------: | --------------: | ------- |
| Outlet temperature | 91°C | 70°C to 85°C | Above 90°C | Above 95°C | Warning |
| Pressure ratio | 3.95 | 3.2 to 3.8 | Above 3.9 | Above 4.1 | Warning |
| Estimated RUL | 36 cycles | Above 50 cycles | Below 40 cycles | Below 25 cycles | Warning |

---

## 4. Findings

* Outlet temperature increased to 91°C.
* Normal outlet temperature range is 70°C to 85°C.
* Pressure ratio instability was observed during loaded operation.
* Estimated Remaining Useful Life trend is decreasing.
* Filter replacement was planned, but verification evidence is delayed.
* Compressor inspection should be planned during the next maintenance window.

---

## 5. Risk Interpretation

C-201 is considered medium risk because multiple warning-level indicators are present.

The current condition may indicate:

* Compressor degradation
* Filter restriction
* Increased operating load
* Pressure instability
* Need for planned maintenance

There is no immediate critical failure evidence, but maintenance planning is required.

---

## 6. Recommended Action

The maintenance team should:

1. Verify filter replacement completion.
2. Review RUL trend report.
3. Check outlet temperature trend.
4. Check pressure ratio stability.
5. Schedule compressor inspection during the next maintenance window.
6. Follow safe shutdown procedure before physical inspection.

---

## 7. Related Documents

* SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
* SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* work_orders.csv
* sensor_readings.csv

---

## 8. Keywords

C-201, compressor, monthly inspection, RUL, remaining useful life, outlet temperature, pressure ratio, filter replacement, delayed verification, maintenance planning

# IR-GEN-001: Safety Evidence Audit

Document ID: IR-GEN-001
Asset ID: GENERAL
Asset Name: PlantMind Demo Assets
Asset Type: General Audit
Document Type: Inspection Report
Inspection Date: 2026-03-10
Inspector: Demo Safety Auditor
Inspection Status: Completed

---

## 1. Audit Purpose

This audit was performed to review maintenance and compliance evidence availability for the PlantMind demo assets.

The audit covers:

* P-101 Cooling Water Circulation Pump
* C-201 Process Air Compressor
* HX-301 Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger

The purpose is to identify missing, delayed, or overdue evidence before compliance review.

---

## 2. Audit Scope

The audit reviewed the following evidence categories:

* Vibration inspection evidence
* Lubrication evidence
* Work permit evidence
* Monthly compressor inspection evidence
* Filter replacement verification
* Pressure test evidence
* Cleaning and fouling inspection evidence
* LOTO evidence

---

## 3. Evidence Review Summary

| Asset ID | Evidence Requirement | Evidence Status | Notes |
| -------- | ------------------------------------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| P-101 | Weekly vibration inspection | Available | IR-P101-001 is available |
| P-101 | Weekly lubrication check | Missing | No lubrication record found for current week |
| P-101 | Work permit before bearing inspection | Missing | No work permit record attached |
| C-201 | Monthly compressor inspection | Available | IR-C201-001 is available |
| C-201 | Filter replacement verification | Delayed | WO-1003 indicates delayed verification |
| C-201 | Safe shutdown procedure | Available | SOP-C201-001 is available |
| HX-301 | Pressure test evidence | Available | Pressure test SOP reference is available |
| HX-301 | Cleaning and fouling inspection | Overdue | Cleaning evidence

essure test evidence | Available | Pressure test SOP reference is available |
| HX-301 | Cleaning and fouling inspection | Overdue | Cleaning evidence not available for current cycle |
| HX-301 | Work permit before cleaning | Missing | No work permit record attached |

---

## 4. Audit Findings

The audit found five compliance evidence gaps:

1. P-101 weekly lubrication evidence is missing.
2. P-101 work permit evidence is missing before bearing inspection.
3. C-201 filter replacement verification is delayed.
4. HX-301 cleaning and fouling inspection evidence is overdue.
5. HX-301 work permit evidence is missing before cleaning activity.

---

## 5. Risk Interpretation

Missing maintenance evidence can increase operational and compliance risk.

P-101 has both technical risk and compliance risk because high vibration has been detected, but lubrication and work permit evidence are missing.

C-201 has medium compliance risk because filter replacement verification is delayed.

HX-301 has compliance risk because cleaning evidence is overdue and work permit evidence is missing.

---

## 6. Recommended Action

The maintenance and compliance teams should:

1. Upload or complete P-101 lubrication record.
2. Attach work permit evidence for P-101 bearing inspection.
3. Verify C-201 filter replacement completion.
4. Schedule HX-301 cleaning and fouling inspection.
5. Attach work permit evidence before HX-301 cleaning activity.
6. Review all open work orders before final compliance review.

---

## 7. Related Documents

* SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
* SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
* SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
* SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* work_orders.csv
* ground_truth_compliance_gaps.csv

---

## 8. Keywords

safety audit, compliance evidence, missing evidence, delayed verification, overdue cleaning, work permit, LOTO, P-101, C-201, HX-301

# IR-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Performance Inspection

Document ID: IR-HX301-001
Asset ID: HX-301
Asset Name: Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger
Asset Type: Heat Exchanger
Document Type: Inspection Report
Inspection Date: 2026-03-05
Inspector: Demo Inspector
Inspection Status: Completed

---

## 1. Inspection Purpose

This inspection was performed to evaluate the performance condition of HX-301 Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger after reduced outlet temperature was observed during process operation.

The inspection focused on outlet temperature, pressure drop, heat transfer efficiency, fouling symptoms, and cleaning evidence availability.

---

## 2. Inspection Scope

The inspection covered:

* Outlet temperature performance
* Pressure drop across heat exchanger
* Heat transfer efficiency indication
* Cleaning record availability
* Fouling suspicion
* Need for cleaning inspection

---

## 3. Measurement Summary

| Parameter | Measured Value | Normal Limit | Warning Limit | Critical Limit | Status |
| ------------------ | -------------: | ------------: | ------------: | -------------: | ------- |
| Outlet temperature | 88°C | 90°C or above | Below 90°C | Below 85°C | Warning |
| Pressure drop | 1.9 bar | Below 1.2 bar | Above 1.8 bar | Above 2.2 bar | Warning |
| Efficiency index | 0.71 | Above 0.85 | Below 0.80 | Below 0.70 | Warning |

---

## 4. Findings

* Outlet temperature was recorded at 88°C, which is below the acceptable target.
* Pressure drop was recorded at 1.9 bar, which is above the warning threshold.
* Heat transfer efficiency index is below the normal range.
* Fouling or restricted flow is suspected.
* Cleaning evidence is overdue for the current inspection cycle.
* Cleaning inspection should be scheduled.

---

## 5. Risk Interpretation

HX-301 is considered medium risk because performance indicators suggest reduced heat transfer efficiency.

The current condition may indicate:

* Tube-side fouling
* Shell-side fouling
* Flow restriction
* Delayed cleaning
* Process-side operating variation

Cleaning evidence must be reviewed because the cleaning record is overdue.

---

## 6. Recommended Action

The maintenance team should:

1. Schedule cleaning inspection.
2. Review last cleaning record.
3. Check outlet temperature trend.
4. Check pressure drop trend.
5. Inspect tube-side condition if accessible.
6. Attach cleaning evidence after maintenance.
7. Review work permit before cleaning activity.

---

## 7. Related Documents

* SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
* SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
* INC-HX301-001: Low Heat Transfer Efficiency
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* work_orders.csv
* sensor_readings.csv

---

## 8. Keywords

HX-301, heat exchanger, performance inspection, outlet temperature, pressure drop, efficiency index, fouling, cleaning overdue, cleaning evidence, maintenance

# IR-P101-001: Pump Vibration Inspection

Document ID: IR-P101-001
Asset ID: P-101
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump
Asset Type: Pump
Document Type: Inspection Report
Inspection Date: 2026-01-21
Inspector: Demo Inspector
Inspection Status: Completed

---

## 1. Inspection Purpose

This inspection was performed to evaluate the vibration condition of P-101 Cooling Water Circulation Pump after abnormal vibration was reported during normal operation.

---

## 2. Inspection Scope

The inspection covered:

* Pump bearing housing vibration
* Visible mounting condition
* Operator-reported vibration behavior
* Need for follow-up bearing inspection
* Need for lubrication verification

---

## 3. Measurement Summary

| Parameter | Measured Value | Normal Limit | Warning Limit | Critical Limit | Status |
| --------- | -------------: | -------------: | ------------: | -------------: | -------- |
| Vibration | 7.8 mm/s | Below 4.5 mm/s | 6.0 mm/s | 7.5 mm/s | Critical |

The measured vibration exceeded the critical threshold.

---

## 4. Findings

* Vibration reading was recorded at 7.8 mm/s.
* Critical vibration threshold for P-101 is 7.5 mm/s.
* Abnormal vibration was observed near the bearing housing.
* Operator reported intermittent vibration during normal load.
* Bearing inspection is recommended.
* Shaft alignment check is recommended.
* Lubrication verification is required.

---

## 5. Risk Interpretation

P-101 should be treated as high risk because the vibration value exceeded the critical threshold.

High vibration may indicate:

* Bearing wear
* Lubrication degradation
* Shaft misalignment
* Loose mounting
* Mechanical imbalance

---

## 6. Recommended Action

Create an urgent maintenance work order for:

1. Bearing housing inspection.
2. Lubrication verification.
3. Shaft alignment check.
4. Mounting bolt inspection.
5. Follow-up vibration measurement after corrective action.

---

## 7. Related Documents

* SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
* SOP-P101-002: Pump Vibration Inspection
* INC-P101-001: High Vibration Event
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist

---

## 8. Keywords

P-101, pump, high vibration, critical vibration, bearing housing, bearing wear, lubrication verification, shaft alignment, inspection report

# IR-P101-002: Pump Bearing Temperature Check

Document ID: IR-P101-002
Asset ID: P-101
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump
Asset Type: Pump
Document Type: Inspection Report
Inspection Date: 2026-01-22
Inspector: Demo Inspector
Inspection Status: Completed

---

## 1. Inspection Purpose

This inspection was performed to evaluate the bearing temperature condition of P-101 after high vibration and abnormal bearing noise were reported.

---

## 2. Inspection Scope

The inspection covered:

* Bearing housing temperature
* Bearing noise observation
* Lubrication evidence availability
* Need for corrective maintenance
* Risk of bearing wear

---

## 3. Measurement Summary

| Parameter | Measured Value | Normal Limit | Warning Limit | Critical Limit | Status |
| ------------------- | -------------: | -----------: | ------------: | -------------: | ------- |
| Bearing temperature | 78°C | Below 65°C | 75°C | 80°C | Warning |

The bearing temperature is below the critical limit but close to the maximum allowed value.

---

## 4. Findings

* Bearing temperature was recorded at 78°C.
* Critical bearing temperature limit for P-101 is 80°C.
* Abnormal bearing noise was reported in the previous maintenance observation.
* Lubrication evidence for the current week was not available.
* High vibration was already reported in IR-P101-001.
* Bearing wear or lubrication degradation is suspected.

---

## 5. Risk Interpretation

P-101 requires maintenance attention because elevated bearing temperature, high vibration, abnormal bearing noise, and missing lubrication evidence are present together.

This combination may indicate:

* Lubrication degradation
* Bearing wear
* Shaft misalignment
* Excess friction in bearing housing
* Delayed lubrication inspection

---

## 6. Recommended Action

The maintenance team should:

1. Verify lubrication level.
2. Check lubricant quality.
3. Inspect bearing housing.
4. Review vibration inspection report IR-P101-001.
5. Check shaft alignment.
6. Create or update urgent maintenance work order.
7. Upload lubrication evidence after inspection.

---

## 7. Related Documents

* SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
* SOP-P101-002: Pump Vibration Inspection
* IR-P101-001: Pump Vibration Inspection
* INC-P101-001: High Vibration Event
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist

---

## 8. Keywords

P-101, pump, bearing temperature, elevated temperature, bearing noise, lubrication evidence, bearing wear, vibration, inspection report

chunks.section_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
# SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation

Document ID: SOP-C201-001
Asset ID: C-201
Asset Name: Process Air Compressor
Asset Type: Compressor
Document Type: SOP
Revision: Rev 1.0
Effective Date: 2026-01-01
Owner: Maintenance Department

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 1. Purpose

This SOP defines the safe shutdown and inspection preparation procedure for C-201 Process Air Compressor.

The purpose of this procedure is to ensure safe compressor isolation before maintenance, inspection, filter verification, or internal checks.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 2. Scope

This SOP applies only to:

Asset ID: C-201
Asset Name: Process Air Compressor
Asset Type: Compressor
Location: Compression Area - Train B

This SOP does not apply to P-101 Pump or HX-301 Heat Exchanger.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 3. Maintenance Triggers

C-201 must be reviewed for maintenance if any of the following conditions are observed:

* Remaining Useful Life trend is decreasing.
* Outlet temperature exceeds 90°C.
* Pressure ratio becomes unstable.
* Filter replacement verification is delayed.
* Abnormal compressor noise is reported.
* Monthly inspection identifies abnormal operating behavior.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 4. Safety Requirements

Before compressor inspection or maintenance:

1. Notify operations team.
2. Obtain maintenance work permit.
3. Reduce compressor load gradually.
4. Isolate compressor from process line.
5. Depressurize compressor section.
6. Verify temperature and pressure stabilization.
7. Apply lockout/tagout.
8. Confirm zero-energy state before maintenance.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 5. Normal Operating Criteria

| Parameter | Normal Range | Warning Limit | Critical Limit |
| ------------------ | --------------: | -------------------: | ------------------------: |
| Outlet temperature | 70°C to 85°C | Above 90°C | Above 95°C |
| Pressure ratio | 3.2 to 3.8 | Above 3.9 | Above 4.1 |
| Estimated RUL | Above 50 cycles | Below 40 cycles | Below 25 cycles |
| Filter condition | Verified | Verification delayed | Filter blockage suspected |

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 6. Shutdown Procedure

1. Inform control room before shutdown.
2. Reduce compressor load gradually.
3. Stop compressor according to operating procedure.
4. Isolate inlet and outlet lines.
5. Depressurize compressor section.
6. Confirm pressure is within safe range.
7. Allow equipment temperature to stabilize.
8. Apply LOTO.
9. Record shutdown confirmation.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 7. Inspection Preparation

After safe shutdown:

1. Review latest RUL trend.
2. Review outlet temperature trend.
3. Check pressure ratio history.
4. Verify filter replacement record.
5. Inspect compressor stage if degradation trend is present.
6. Create or update maintenance work order.
7. Attach inspection evidence.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 8. Required Evidence

The following evidence must be maintained:

| Requirement | Evidence Required | Frequency |
| ------------------------------- | ----------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| Monthly compressor inspection | Inspection report | Monthly |
| Filter replacement verification | Maintenance log or work order | As scheduled |
| RUL trend review | RUL report | During maintenance planning |
| Safe shutdown confirmation | Shutdown checklist | Before inspection |
| Work permit | Work permit record | Before maintenance |
| LOTO confirmation | LOTO record | Before equipment opening |

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 9. Recommended Action for Abnormal Condition

If C-201 shows decreasing RUL, high outlet temperature, or pressure instability:

1. Schedule compressor inspection during the next maintenance window.
2. Verify filter replacement completion.
3. Review compressor operating conditions.
4. Check outlet temperature trend.
5. Check pressure ratio trend.
6. Review RUL report before maintenance planning.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 10. Related Documents

* IR-C201-001: Compressor Monthly Inspection
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
* work_orders.csv
* sensor_readings.csv

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
chunks.chunk_text:
## 11. Keywords

C-201, compressor, safe shutdown, RUL, remaining useful life, outlet temperature, pressure ratio, filter replacement, inspection, maintenance, work permit, LOTO

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.section_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
# SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements

Document ID: SOP-GEN-001
Asset ID: GENERAL
Asset Name: Plant Maintenance Safety Procedure
Asset Type: General Safety
Document Type: SOP
Revision: Rev 1.0
Effective Date: 2026-01-01
Owner: Safety and Maintenance Department

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 1. Purpose

This SOP defines the general work permit and lockout tagout requirements for maintenance activities performed on PlantMind demo assets.

The purpose of this procedure is to ensure that maintenance work is controlled, documented, and performed only after required safety evidence is available.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 2. Scope

This SOP applies to maintenance work involving:

* P-101 Cooling Water Circulation Pump
* C-201 Process Air Compressor
* HX-301 Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger

This SOP applies to inspection, cleaning, equipment opening, bearing inspection, compressor inspection, pressure-related maintenance, and isolation activities.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 3. Work Permit Requirement

A maintenance work permit is required before:

1. Opening pump bearing housing.
2. Performing compressor shutdown and inspection.
3. Cleaning or opening heat exchanger sections.
4. Performing pressure-related inspection.
5. Performing maintenance that requires equipment isolation.
6. Performing work that may expose personnel to mechanical, electrical, pressure, or thermal hazards.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 4. Lockout Tagout Requirement

Lockout tagout is required before:

1. Physical inspection of P-101 bearing housing.
2. Maintenance on C-201 compressor.
3. Cleaning or opening HX-301 heat exchanger.
4. Electrical isolation.
5. Mechanical isolation.
6. Depressurization and equipment opening.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 5. Required Evidence

The following evidence must be recorded before maintenance work begins:

| Requirement | Evidence Required |
| --------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| Work permit | Work permit number and approval |
| LOTO confirmation | Lockout tagout checklist |
| Asset identification | Asset ID and asset name |
| Maintenance reason | Inspection, cleaning, fault, or planned maintenance |
| Supervisor approval | Name and timestamp |
| Technician assignment | Technician name |
| Safety confirmation | Isolation and zero-energy confirmation |

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 6. Minimum Evidence Fields

Every work permit or LOTO evidence record must include:

* Asset ID
* Work order ID
* Permit number
* Date
* Technician name
* Supervisor name
* Isolation confirmation
* LOTO confirmation
* Work description
* Approval status

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text: ## 7. Asset-Specific Safety Notes

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
### P-101 Pump

For P-101 bearing inspection:

* Work permit is required before opening bearing housing.
* LOTO is required before physical inspection.
* Lubrication evidence must be reviewed before maintenance.
* Vibration inspection evidence should be reviewed before corrective action.

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
### C-201 Compressor

For C-201 compressor inspection:

* Compressor must be safely shut down.
* System must be depressurized.
* Outlet temperature and pressure must stabilize.
* Filter replacement verification should be reviewed.

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
### HX-301 Heat Exchanger

For HX-301 cleaning inspection:

* Equipment must be isolated.
* Equipment must be depressurized.
* Cleaning work requires work permit evidence.
* Cleaning evidence must be recorded after maintenance.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 8. Compliance Review

A maintenance activity is considered non-compliant if:

* Work permit evidence is missing.
* LOTO evidence is missing.
* Required inspection evidence is missing.
* Required cleaning evidence is overdue.
* Filter replacement verification is delayed.
* Lubrication evidence is missing.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 9. Related Documents

* SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
* SOP-P101-002: Pump Vibration Inspection
* SOP-C201-001: Compressor Safe Shutdown and Inspection Preparation
* SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* work_orders.csv

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
chunks.chunk_text:
## 10. Keywords

work permit, LOTO, lockout tagout, safety, maintenance evidence, compliance, P-101, C-201, HX-301, inspection, cleaning, shutdown, bearing inspection

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.section_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
# SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check

Document ID: SOP-HX301-001
Asset ID: HX-301
Asset Name: Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger
Asset Type: Heat Exchanger
Document Type: SOP
Revision: Rev 1.0
Effective Date: 2026-01-01
Owner: Maintenance Department

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 1. Purpose

This SOP defines the cleaning and fouling inspection procedure for HX-301 Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger.

The purpose of this procedure is to detect reduced heat transfer efficiency, identify fouling symptoms, and ensure cleaning evidence is recorded on schedule.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 2. Scope

This SOP applies only to:

Asset ID: HX-301
Asset Name: Feed Preheater Heat Exchanger
Asset Type: Shell and Tube Heat Exchanger
Location: Process Area - Unit C

This SOP does not apply to P-101 Pump or C-201 Compressor.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 3. Fouling Indicators

HX-301 must be reviewed for fouling if any of the following conditions are observed:

* Outlet temperature falls below 90°C.
* Pressure drop exceeds 1.8 bar.
* Heat transfer efficiency decreases.
* Cleaning evidence is overdue.
* Flow restriction is suspected.
* Previous inspection indicates tube-side fouling.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 4. Inspection Frequency

Cleaning and fouling inspection must be performed:

Frequency: Every 30 days
Evidence Required: Yes
Evidence Type: Cleaning record or fouling inspection report

Every cleaning or fouling inspection must have recorded evidence.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 5. Normal Operating Criteria

| Parameter | Normal Limit | Warning Limit | Critical Limit |
| ------------------ | -------------: | -------------: | -------------: |
| Outlet temperature | 90°C or above | Below 90°C | Below 85°C |
| Pressure drop | Below 1.2 bar | Above 1.8 bar | Above 2.2 bar |
| Efficiency index | Above 0.85 | Below 0.80 | Below 0.70 |
| Cleaning interval | Within 30 days | Beyond 30 days | Beyond 45 days |

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 6. Safety Requirements

Before heat exchanger cleaning or inspection:

1. Obtain maintenance work permit.
2. Isolate heat exchanger from process line.
3. Depressurize equipment before opening.
4. Confirm equipment is drained and cooled.
5. Follow lockout/tagout procedure.
6. Wear required PPE.
7. Confirm safe access before inspection.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 7. Inspection Procedure

1. Review latest outlet temperature trend.
2. Review latest pressure drop trend.
3. Check last cleaning record.
4. Inspect for fouling symptoms.
5. Verify tube-side and shell-side condition if accessible.
6. Record cleaning evidence after completion.
7. Escalate if outlet temperature is below 90°C and pressure drop is above 1.8 bar.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 8. Required Evidence

The following evidence must be maintained:

| Requirement | Evidence Required | Frequency |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------------- |
| Cleaning and fouling inspection | Cleaning record | Every 30 days |
| Pressure drop review | Inspection report | During performance inspection |
| Outlet temperature review | Inspection report | During performance inspection |
| Work permit | Work permit record | Before cleaning |
| LOTO confirmation | LOTO record | Before equipment opening |

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 9. Recommended Action for Abnormal Condition

If HX-301 shows low outlet temperature, high pressure drop, or suspected fouling:

1. Schedule cleaning inspection.
2. Review latest heat exchanger performance inspection.
3. Check pressure drop trend.
4. Check outlet temperature trend.
5. Verify last cleaning record.
6. Attach cleaning evidence after maintenance.
7. Review work permit evidence before cleaning.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 10. Related Documents

* IR-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Performance Inspection
* INC-HX301-001: Low Heat Transfer Efficiency
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist
* SOP-GEN-001: Work Permit and Lockout Tagout Requirements
* work_orders.csv
* sensor_readings.csv

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-HX301-001: Heat Exchanger Cleaning and Fouling Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 11. Keywords

HX-301, heat exchanger, fouling, cleaning, outlet temperature, pressure drop, efficiency index, cleaning evidence, inspection, compliance, work permit, LOTO

chunks.document_title: SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
chunks.section_title: SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
chunks.chunk_text:
# SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check

Document ID: SOP-P101-001 
Asset ID: P-101 
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump 
Asset Type: Pump 
Document Type: SOP 
Revision: Rev 1.0 
Effective Date: 2026-01-01 
Owner: Maintenance Department 

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 1. Purpose

This procedure defines the weekly lubrication and bearing inspection requirements for P-101 Cooling Water Circulation Pump.

The purpose of this SOP is to ensure that the pump bearing system remains properly lubricated, abnormal bearing noise is detected early, and vibration-related failures are prevented.

---

chunks.document_title: SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
chunks.chunk_text:
## 2. Scope

This SOP applies only to:

```text
Asset ID: P-101
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump
Asset Type: Centrifugal Pump
Location: Utility Area - Line A

# SOP-P101-002: Pump Vibration Inspection

Document ID: SOP-P101-002
Asset ID: P-101
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump
Asset Type: Pump
Document Type: SOP
Revision: Rev 1.0
Effective Date: 2026-01-01
Owner: Maintenance Department

---

## 1. Purpose

This SOP defines the vibration inspection procedure for P-101 Cooling Water Circulation Pump.

The purpose of this procedure is to detect abnormal vibration early, identify possible bearing or alignment issues, and prevent unplanned pump failure.

---

## 2. Scope

This SOP applies only to:

Asset ID: P-101
Asset Name: Cooling Water Circulation Pump
Asset Type: Centrifugal Pump
Location: Utility Area - Line A

This SOP does not apply to C-201 Compressor or HX-301 Heat Exchanger.

---

## 3. Vibration Thresholds

| Condition | Vibration Reading |
| --------- | -------------------: |
| Normal | Below 4.5 mm/s |
| Warning | 4.5 mm/s to 7.5 mm/s |
| Critical | Above 7.5 mm/s |

If vibration exceeds 7.5 mm/s, the issue must be escalated immediately.

---

## 4. Inspection Frequency

P-101 vibration inspection must be performed as part of routine condition monitoring.

Minimum inspection frequency:

Weekly vibration check.

Additional inspection is required when abnormal bearing noise, high bearing temperature, or unusual pump behavior is reported.

---

## 5. Inspection Procedure

1. Verify that the pump is operating under normal load.
2. Collect vibration reading near the bearing housing.
3. Compare the reading against vibration thresholds.
4. Record vibration value in the inspection report.
5. Check for abnormal bearing noise.
6. Check for visible looseness or mounting issues.
7. Review lubrication evidence.
8. Escalate if vibration exceeds warning or critical limit.

---

## 6. Required Evidence

Every vibration inspection must record:

* Asset ID
* Inspection date
* Technician name
* Vibration reading
* Operating condition
* Bearing noise observation
* Recommended action
* Linked work order if abnormal

---

## 7. Abnormal Condition Criteria

P-101 should be treated as abnormal if any of the following are found:

* Vibration reading is above 6.0 mm/s.
* Vibration reading is above 7.5 mm/s.
* Abnormal bearing noise is observed.
* Bearing temperature is close to 80°C.
* Lubrication evidence is missing.
* Shaft misalignment is suspected.

---

## 8. Recommended Action

If vibration is above warning threshold:

1. Continue monitoring.
2. Check bearing noise.
3. Verify lubrication evidence.
4. Check alignment during next maintenance window.

If vibration is above critical threshold:

1. Create urgent maintenance work order.
2. Inspect bearing housing.
3. Verify lubrication level and lubricant quality.
4. Check shaft alignment.
5. Inspect mounting bolts.
6. Review incident history.

---

## 9. Related Documents

* SOP-P101-001: Pump Lubrication and Bearing Check
* IR-P101-001: Pump Vibration Inspection Report
* IR-P101-002: Pump Bearing Temperature Check
* INC-P101-001: High Vibration Event
* COMP-001: PlantMind Demo Compliance Checklist

---

## 10. Keywords

P-101, pump, vibration, bearing housing, vibration threshold, high vibration, bearing noise, inspection, maintenance, compliance evidence
# AI4I Mapping for PlantMind AI

## Purpose

This folder contains the AI4I 2020 Predictive Maintenance dataset used as a public reference for machine failure and predictive maintenance behavior.

PlantMind AI maps this dataset to the P-101 Pump demo use case.

## PlantMind Asset Mapping

| AI4I Concept | PlantMind AI Mapping |
|---|---|
| Machine condition | P-101 Pump condition |
| Machine failure | Pump failure risk |
| Rotational speed / torque / temperature | Pump operating condition signals |
| Failure label | Maintenance risk indicator |

## PlantMind Demo Use

The dataset supports this PlantMind AI story:

P-101 Pump is high risk because vibration increases above threshold, abnormal bearing noise is reported, and lubrication evidence is missing.

## Important Note

The PlantMind AI Day 2 demo already contains simplified sensor values in:

```text
data/raw/structured/sensor_readings.csv

Data Set: FD001
Train trjectories: 100
Test trajectories: 100
Conditions: ONE (Sea Level)
Fault Modes: ONE (HPC Degradation)

Data Set: FD002
Train trjectories: 260
Test trajectories: 259
Conditions: SIX 
Fault Modes: ONE (HPC Degradation)

Data Set: FD003
Train trjectories: 100
Test trajectories: 100
Conditions: ONE (Sea Level)
Fault Modes: TWO (HPC Degradation, Fan Degradation)

Data Set: FD004
Train trjectories: 248
Test trajectories: 249
Conditions: SIX 
Fault Modes: TWO (HPC Degradation, Fan Degradation)

Experimental Scenario

Data sets consists of multiple multivariate time series. Each data set is further divided into training and test subsets. Each time series is from a different engine � i.e., the data can be considered to be from a fleet of engines of the same type. Each engine starts with different degrees of initial wear and manufacturing variation which is unknown to the

rom a fleet of engines of the same type. Each engine starts with different degrees of initial wear and manufacturing variation which is unknown to the user. This wear and variation is considered normal, i.e., it is not considered a fault condition. There are three operational settings that have a substantial effect on engine performance. These settings are also included in the data. The data is contaminated with sensor noise.

The engine is operating normally at the start of each time series, and develops a fault at some point during the series. In the training set, the fault grows in magnitude until system failure. In the test set, the time series ends some time prior to system failure. The objective of the competition is to predict the number of remaining operational cycles before failure in the test set, i.e., the number of operational cycles after the last cycle that the engine will

umber of remaining operational cycles before failure in the test set, i.e., the number of operational cycles after the last cycle that the engine will continue to operate. Also provided a vector of true Remaining Useful Life (RUL) values for the test data.

The data are provided as a zip-compressed text file with 26 columns of numbers, separated by spaces. Each row is a snapshot of data taken during a single operational cycle, each column is a different variable. The columns correspond to:
1) unit number
2) time, in cycles
3) operational setting 1
4) operational setting 2
5) operational setting 3
6) sensor measurement 1
7) sensor measurement 2
...
26) sensor measurement 26

Reference: A. Saxena, K. Goebel, D. Simon, and N. Eklund, �Damage Propagation Modeling for Aircraft Engine Run-to-Failure Simulation�, in the Proceedings of the Ist International Conference on Prognostics and Health
Propagation Modeling for Aircraft Engine Run-to-Failure Simulation�, in the Proceedings of the Ist International Conference on Prognostics and Health Management (PHM08), Denver CO, Oct 2008.

chunks.document_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.section_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.chunk_text: # NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI

chunks.document_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.chunk_text:
## Purpose

This folder contains NASA C-MAPSS public dataset files used as a reference for turbomachinery degradation and Remaining Useful Life behavior.

PlantMind AI maps this dataset to the C-201 Process Air Compressor demo use case.

chunks.document_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.chunk_text:
## Selected Dataset for MVP

For the PlantMind AI MVP, only FD001 will be used.

chunks.document_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.chunk_text:
## Why FD001

FD001 is selected because it is the simplest subset:

- 100 training trajectories
- 100 test trajectories
- One operating condition
- One fault mode
- Fault mode: HPC degradation

This makes it suitable for a hackathon MVP and avoids unnecessary complexity.

chunks.document_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.chunk_text:
## PlantMind Asset Mapping

NASA C-MAPSS FD001 is mapped as follows:

| NASA C-MAPSS Concept | PlantMind AI Mapping |
|---|---|
| Engine unit | C-201 Compressor |
| Operational cycle | Compressor operating cycle |
| Sensor measurements | Compressor condition signals |
| RUL value | Estimated remaining useful life |
| Degradation trend | Compressor maintenance risk |

chunks.document_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.chunk_text:
## PlantMind Demo Use

The dataset supports this PlantMind AI story:

C-201 Compressor has a decreasing Remaining Useful Life trend. The system recommends compressor inspection and filter replacement verification during the next maintenance window.

chunks.document_title: NASA C-MAPSS Mapping for PlantMind AI
chunks.chunk_text:
## Files Used

The MVP will use:

```text
train_FD001.txt
test_FD001.txt
RUL_FD001.txt

# Public Dataset and Standards References

This folder records public references used for PlantMind AI demo dataset design.

## 1. AI4I 2020 Predictive Maintenance Dataset

AI4I is used as a public predictive maintenance reference.

### PlantMind Mapping

AI4I-style predictive maintenance behavior is mapped to:

- Asset: P-101 Pump
- Demo signal: vibration increase
- Failure context: bearing risk / machine failure risk
- PlantMind feature: maintenance intelligence and sensor-risk explanation

### Current Use

PlantMind AI already contains simplified synthetic sensor values in:

```text
data/raw/structured/sensor_readings.csv
